methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Galadyne is a pre-seed stage defense and security company founded by Chandler Luzsicza that has raised $4.8M to date. The broader defense and security sector comprises 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI—though same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within this universe are sparse.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised ($4.8M, rounded to $5M as a valuation proxy) and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for pre-seed-to-next-stage advancement yields $12M. This method directly reflects the company's funding history and market-standard stage progression economics.

The peer-median approach is not applicable. While 21 same-sector peers exist in defense and security, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies yields insufficient datapoints (fewer than three required for reliable benchmarking). A broader sector-wide median would not meaningfully constrain the valuation.

The triangulation analysis, derived from the single applicable method, supports a range of $10M to $14M with a median of $12M. The primary uncertainty stems from limited same-stage peer visibility in this sector, making stage-progression progression the dominant signal. Market traction metrics and product development status would materially inform positioning within this range.
